behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between request-builder and API client. A unit test of string/list formatting could miss wiring bugs where labels are dropped before send. Rejected System: behavior only says request content (`includes the complete list`), not real network/OS effects. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on model judgment or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classification module to a stub transport/client and assert outbound payload contains every configured taxonomy label on each call.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure can occur at module boundary: API response parsing + taxonomy validation + returned value. A pure unit test of validator logic may miss that the module forwards raw provider output. Rejected Agentic: behavior is explicitly about out-of-taxonomy handling, not quality of model choice. Rejected System: simulated API response is sufficient; no real OS/network behavior required.
behaviors.test_description: Use module with a stubbed API response containing an invalid label; assert caller receives `unknown` and never the raw invalid label.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic normalization logic over identical input; one local function/object boundary with no I/O. Rejected Integration: behavior text scopes to `normalization step` itself, not cross-module handoff. Rejected higher levels: no real infra, no model dependence, no multi-step pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and assert identical normalized label each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires `repeated live API calls` and consistency of returned labels, which depends on real model outputs that may vary run-to-run. A mock would hide model-driven inconsistency. Rejected Workflow: repeated calls are not a chained multi-agent pipeline; each call is a single agentic decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real provider classifications for identical input, record raw/provider outputs and final labels, and assert label drift only occurs when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is policy enforcement plus dispatch gating (builder + budget checker + sender). Unit tests of token math alone can miss bugs where over-budget requests still dispatch. Rejected System: behavior says `before dispatch`; can be validated with in-process stub client. Rejected Agentic: no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a low budget, invoke module with oversized input, and assert truncate/reject policy is applied and outbound dispatch follows policy expectations (sent truncated or blocked).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real AI provider call and provider-reported usage; mocked usage can trivially pass and hide real token behavior. Even with deterministic assertion (usage <= budget), outcome depends on actual model/provider token accounting and generated completion characteristics. Rejected Workflow: single call measurement, not multi-step chained agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative documents, capture provider usage metadata, and assert each call’s reported total tokens is within configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration across modules (error mapping, wait/backoff, retry, success propagation). In-process stubs can reproduce rate-limit responses and verify behavior. Rejected System: no evidence this needs real network timing/OS primitives to expose failure. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: correctness does not depend on model judgment content.
behaviors.test_description: Stub API to return rate-limit error then success; assert module performs wait/backoff and retry sequence and returns successful label to caller.
